body.title: pe_chain HYPOTHESIZE: actual[:4000] truncates real targets out — LLM hallucinates from prior
body.result: Closed two-part fix: (1) actual char cap 4000→16000 restored grounding (cmd_claim now in LLM context); (2) _strip_line_prefix removes line-number prefixes when serializing for the LLM (LLM no longer has to mentally strip prefix → preserves indent and quote style). 23 new unit tests pin the prompt-assembly behavior. Cert walk-02 attempt 7 follows as live integration test.
body.description:
Cert walk-02 substitute attempt 4 surfaced the actual root cause of the paraphrase pattern: pe_hypothesize caps `actual` at 4000 chars before sending to the LLM. For mid-to-large files (e.g. cc_queue.py at 750 lines / ~30KB), the OBSERVE step's 11428-char window is truncated to 4000 chars — the relevant target (cmd_claim at offset 10084) is cut off entirely. The LLM is asked to modify cmd_claim while seeing only lines 1-94 of the file. With nothing real to copy, it hallucinates a plausible Python pattern from prior (`if t['status'] == 'pending':`) — and on retry, sees the same truncated window, hallucinates the same prior, locks in.

This explains the 'paraphrase stickiness' filed earlier today (T-pe-chain-llm-paraphrase-stickiness) — it wasn't model stickiness, it was model-generates-from-prior-when-target-not-in-prompt.

Fix: bump `actual[:4000]` to `_HYPOTHESIZE_ACTUAL_CHAR_CAP=16000` (constant) and apply consistently in initial prompt and retry prompt. Modern LLMs (qwen-2.5-coder, haiku, sonnet) handle 16K context comfortably; the 4000-char cap was a defensive measure for a smaller model era and now defeats the OBSERVE window-widening shipped earlier today (T-pe-chain-observe-window-fix).

This is the cert walk-02 root cause we'd been three layers deep on. The OBSERVE fix and retry-feedback fix shipped today are still useful (different concerns) but neither was sufficient because both fed into the same 4000-char bottleneck.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (constant + 2 callsites)
**Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — single cap constant + applying it. OUT — adaptive sizing per model tier; per-file-size cap; rewriting the prompt structure.
**Test plan:** 105 pe_chain regression tests pass. Live integration: cert walk-02 substitute attempt 5 — predicted to produce a verbatim-valid edit on the first attempt (no retries needed) since LLM now actually sees cmd_claim in its prompt.
